plan.title: Document sandbox credential remediation and regression coverage

narratives.Description: Operators need clear guidance when a local sandbox can read gh config bytes but cannot authenticate or reach GitHub. This story documents Cursor sandbox behavior and pins the intended swarm skill guidance without changing the dispatch implementation files owned by earlier slices.
narratives.ImplementationPlan:
1. Update the skills/deft-directive-swarm/SKILL.md file and docs/subagent-heartbeat.md file with the runtime/auth troubleshooting path for full-access execution, trusted command allowlisting, and injected-token handoff.
2. Add pytest verification evidence in tests/content/test_swarm_skill.py and tests/content/test_allocation_context_skills.py to verify the documentation names Cursor sandbox UID remap, host-gh validation, cloud/headless injected-token failure, and remediation options.
narratives.UserStory: As a Deft operator, I want documented remediation for local worker GitHub auth failures, so that I can choose full-access, allowlisting, or injected-token handoff safely.
narratives.Traces: Issue #1557 decomposition recommendation item 4; Issue #1557 Cursor native sandbox comment.

narrative.Acceptance: Given an operator reads the swarm skill guidance, then it explains Cursor sandbox UID remap and does not present sandbox root as host root ownership.
narrative.Traces: Issue #1557 Cursor native sandbox suggested AC.

narrative.Acceptance: Given worker sandbox GitHub auth fails while host auth works, then the docs name full-access execution, trusted gh allowlisting, and injected-token handoff as remediation paths.
Given content tests run, then they fail if the swarm guidance drops runtime-mode classification, host-gh validation, cloud/headless injected-token failure, or sandbox remediation language.

expected_outputs: swarm guidance tests assert sandbox remediation and auth-mode documentation

references.title: Issue #1557: swarm: make GitHub credential policy runtime-aware for local agents
